text.title: Funnel Tactics Basics Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Funnel Tactics Basics` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Each tactic MUST be tagged with funnel stage (TOFU / MOFU / BOFU / Onboarding). Cross-stage tactics are flagged.
rule.rationale: Stage-misapplied tactics fail (BOFU urgency on TOFU traffic doesn't work); explicit tagging enforces fit.

rule.statement: Each tactic MUST cite a quantified lift band (e.g. 5-15% on signup completion) with source + sample. Hand-wave lift claims forbidden.
rule.rationale: Un-quantified lifts mislead prioritisation; the band + source give realistic expectations.

rule.statement: Each pre-launch checklist item MUST be binary-checkable (yes/no) by an external reviewer. Subjective items are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Subjective items collapse into self-approval; binary items force objective verification.

rule.statement: Each checklist MUST name a single owner who runs the pre-launch audit + signs off. Group handles forbidden.
rule.rationale: Group-owned checklists rot at 80% within 60 days; named owner converts to ~70% execution.

rule.statement: Each lift claim MUST link to its source (case study / Nielsen / Baymard / internal experiment). Un-sourced claims are demoted to 'inspiration'.
rule.rationale: Un-sourced lift bands invite challenge; evidence anchors keep the spec credible over time.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
